cases.title: Testing-plan close-time row plus MVP conditional absence alerts: post-close recalculation emits threshold alert
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Close-time absent finalization block applies (FR-09, BR-13, AC-12); MVP conditional scope includes absence threshold alerts per acceptance doc §3.2.
Class section has effective policy absenceThresholdPercent 20 and excusedCountsTowardThreshold false.
Five closed sessions exist; target student has four Absent and one Present (80% unexcused absence rate before next close).
M10 Notification module is enabled for the test environment.
steps: Open a sixth session, leave target student Pending, and invoke M03 closeSession transitioning beforeState Open to afterState Closed.
Verify BR-13 marks the pending student Absent, yielding five Absent of six countable sessions.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId) post-close hook per BR-17 trigger.
Query alert delivery queue or policy_alert_events table for the student-section pair.
Verify attendance_records statuses are unchanged by the alert event itself.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix cross-walk links close-time absent finalization to BR-17: after session close recalculates unexcused absence rate (83.3% > 20% threshold) and emits an informational absence-threshold alert with logged alert event; attendance records are not auto-modified by the alert per BR-17 exception.

cases.title: M10 absence-rate evaluator compares against BR-20 effective policy absenceThresholdPercent
preconditions: Real DB transaction boundary for attendance_policies, attendance_records, and M10 alert persistence.
Institution default absenceThresholdPercent 30; section-scoped override absenceThresholdPercent 20 via attendance_policies row.
Class section with ten closed sessions: student has three Absent (30% rate — below institution default but above section 20%).
steps: Call M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, now) and confirm absenceThresholdPercent 20 sourced from ClassSection per BR-20.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId).
Assert alert generated because 30% exceeds 20% section threshold.
Repeat evaluation using institution-only policy (no section override, threshold 30%) and confirm no alert at 30% rate.
cases.expected: M10 integrates with M06 policy resolution: BR-17 comparison uses effective absenceThresholdPercent (section 20% wins over institution 30%); alert fires only when unexcused rate strictly exceeds the resolved threshold.

cases.title: Excused absences excluded from BR-17 rate when excusedCountsTowardThreshold is false
preconditions: Section policy: absenceThresholdPercent 20, excusedCountsTowardThreshold false.
Ten closed sessions for one enrolled student: two Absent, two Excused, six Present.
M10 evaluator configured per domain model: Excused excluded from unexcused absence calculation when flag is false.
steps: Seed attendance_records with two Absent, two Excused, six Present across ten closed sessions.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId).
Verify computed unexcusedAbsenceRate excludes Excused sessions from numerator per BR-17 condition.
Toggle excusedCountsTowardThreshold true on policy, re-run evaluation, and compare alert outcome.
cases.expected: When excusedCountsTowardThreshold false, Excused sessions are excluded from unexcused absence rate per BR-17; alert decision reflects only unexcused Absent sessions; toggling the flag changes computed rate and may change alert eligibility.

cases.title: Session Open to Closed transition triggers BR-17 post-close absence-threshold recalculation
preconditions: Open class session with one enrolled student remaining Pending.
Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; student already has two Absent of nine prior closed sessions (22.2% after next Absent).
M10 post-close hook subscribed to M03 session-close domain event.
steps: Capture beforeState: session Open, student Pending, no prior absence-threshold alert for this section.
Invoke closeSession(sessionId) transitioning session to Closed and applying BR-13 Absent for pending student.
Capture afterState: session Closed, student attendanceStatus Absent for this session.
Verify M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold runs once post-close and persists alert event with computed rate and threshold snapshot.
cases.expected: beforeState Open → afterState Closed triggers BR-13 absent finalization then BR-17 threshold evaluation; alert event logged with unexcused rate exceeding 20% threshold; no attendance record statuses are auto-modified by the alert itself.

cases.title: Concurrent session closes for same section dedupe BR-17 alerts to avoid noisy duplicates
preconditions: Section with absenceThresholdPercent 20; student already exceeds threshold before test.
Three sessions in Open state for the same class section affecting the same at-risk student.
M10 alert deduplication window or idempotency key on (classSectionId, studentUserId, policyVersion) is configured per MVP conditional scope.
steps: Fire three parallel closeSession commands for distinct session IDs in the same section.
Collect all M10 alert events or notification queue rows for the student-section pair within a 60-second window.
Verify attendance_records reflect three new Absent rows (one per session) without duplication errors.
Assert at most one absence-threshold alert notification is emitted per deduplication policy.
cases.expected: Concurrent closes update attendance correctly while BR-17 alert emission deduplicates per MVP conditional scope ('avoid noisy false alarms'); student receives one informational alert, not three identical notifications for the same threshold breach state.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close returns 200 and emits BR-17 alert auditable via GET /v1/audit-logs
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20 configured via POST /v1/policies.
Student enrolled in section with four Absent of five prior closed sessions.
Sixth session is Open with the student Pending; Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.state Closed and standard {data, meta, error} envelope.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=AbsenceThresholdAlert&targetId={studentUserId}&from={closeTimestamp}.
Inspect audit payload: unexcusedAbsenceRate, absenceThresholdPercent, classSectionId, and recipient list metadata.
cases.expected: Session close HTTP 200 triggers BR-17 alert generation; audit log contains AbsenceThresholdAlert entry with computed rate above 20% threshold, student and section correlation IDs, and notification recipient configuration; attendance records remain unchanged by the alert event.

cases.title: Primary close-session workflow triggers BR-17 notifications to student, lecturer, and academic admin
preconditions: WF-01 complete: section, enrollments, policy absenceThresholdPercent 20, multiple sessions Scheduled.
Lecturer completes sequential open/close cycles leaving target student with repeated Absent outcomes.
M10 notification delivery queue captures student, lecturer, and academic-admin recipients per policy defaults.
steps: Run sequential session open/close cycles until target student unexcused absence rate exceeds 20%.
On threshold-crossing close, verify BR-13 Absent applied for pending student in that session.
Query notification queue for three recipient types: student self, section lecturer, academic admin per BR-17 outcome.
Confirm each notification payload includes section code, student identifier, computed rate, and threshold percent.
Verify attendance ledger reflects only Absent/Present/Late statuses from close processing, not alert side effects.
cases.expected: Primary WF close-session happy path produces BR-17 informational notifications to student, lecturer, and academic admin when threshold is exceeded; alert event is logged; attendance ledger is not auto-modified by the alert.

cases.title: Alternate trigger: scheduled job recalculates absence rate and emits BR-17 alert without new session close
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; student already has unexcused absence rate above threshold from prior closed sessions.
No new session is closing during the test window.
M10 scheduled absence-threshold evaluation job is enabled per BR-17 trigger (scheduled job path).
steps: Record attendance_records beforeState: all statuses unchanged.
Invoke M10 scheduled evaluateAbsenceThresholdBatch(classSectionId) or equivalent cron job.
Query alert events and notification queue for at-risk student.
Re-query attendance_records and confirm no status mutations occurred.
cases.expected: BR-17 scheduled-job trigger recalculates unexcused absence rate and emits informational alert with logged event; attendance records remain identical to beforeState per BR-17 exception (alert is informational only).

cases.title: Student remains below threshold until Nth absent session then BR-17 alert fires on crossing session only
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; ten total sessions planned.
Student starts with zero Absent; each absent session increments rate by 10%.
steps: Close sessions 1–2 with student Absent (20% rate — at threshold boundary).
Verify no absence-threshold alert emitted at exactly 20% because BR-17 condition is exceeds, not meets.
Close session 3 with student Absent (30% rate — exceeds 20%).
Verify alert emits on session 3 close only.
Close session 4 with student Present; verify absence rate recalculates but alert deduplication prevents duplicate notification if still above threshold.
cases.expected: Alternate branch: BR-17 alert fires only when rate strictly exceeds absenceThresholdPercent; threshold crossing on third absent session triggers first alert; subsequent closes while still above threshold do not spam duplicate alerts.

cases.title: Raising absenceThresholdPercent via policy PATCH suppresses BR-17 alert on next evaluation
preconditions: Student unexcused absence rate 22% with policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; alert already emitted.
AcademicAdmin authenticated; section-scoped policy row exists.
Audit query available for policy mutation.
steps: Record beforeState: student rate 22%, threshold 20%, alert active.
AcademicAdmin PATCH /v1/policies/{policyId} changing absenceThresholdPercent from 20 to 25.
Close another session (student Present) and invoke M10 re-evaluation.
Record afterState: rate ~20% (22% of 10 sessions if one more held), threshold 25%.
Query audit logs for policy mutation and verify no new absence-threshold alert for previously alerted student.
cases.expected: Critical policy configuration change updates threshold for subsequent BR-17 evaluations; student at 22% no longer exceeds 25% threshold; no new alert emitted; policy mutation audit captured per BR-22; prior attendance records unchanged.

cases.title: Student denied cross-student absence-threshold data; Lecturer denied unassigned section alerts per BR-19
preconditions: Two class sections A and B each have students exceeding absence threshold.
Student S enrolled only in section A; Lecturer L assigned only to section A.
Valid bearer tokens for Student S, Lecturer L, and Lecturer L2 (assigned to section B only).
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student S calls GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?studentUserId={studentInSectionB}.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden; no other student's rate or alert metadata returned.
Student S calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section B absence-threshold data.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage per BR-18.
Lecturer L calls GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?classSectionId={sectionB}.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope per BR-19.
Lecturer L calls GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?classSectionId={sectionA}; assert HTTP 200 with scoped results only.
cases.expected: RBAC enforces self-only alert read for Student and section-scoped read for Lecturer per BR-19; cross-student and cross-section absence-threshold data denied without partial leakage; student export denied per BR-18.

cases.title: Unexcused rate exactly at absenceThresholdPercent does not emit BR-17 alert; one additional Absent exceeds and emits
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20.00.
Student with exactly 20% unexcused absence rate (e.g. 2 Absent of 10 closed sessions).
M10 evaluator invoked after each session close.
steps: Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold at exactly 20.00% computed rate.
Assert no alert event persisted (BR-17 condition is exceeds, not meets).
Close one additional session with student Absent yielding 27.27% rate (3 of 11).
Re-invoke evaluator and assert alert event created with rate and threshold in payload.
cases.expected: Boundary enforces strict exceed semantics: 20.00% against 20% threshold produces no alert; crossing to 27.27% triggers BR-17 alert generation with logged event.

cases.title: Null or undefined absenceThresholdPercent in effective policy disables BR-17 alert generation
preconditions: Effective policy resolved for section has absenceThresholdPercent null (field undefined at all precedence levels per BR-20).
Student with high unexcused absence rate (e.g. 50% Absent).
M10 notification module enabled globally.
steps: Call M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, now) and confirm absenceThresholdPercent is null.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId) after session close.
Query alert events table for the student-section pair.
Verify no alert row created and no error thrown.
cases.expected: When effective policy omits absenceThresholdPercent, BR-17 alert evaluation is skipped gracefully; no false alerts for institutions that have not configured a threshold; attendance processing unaffected.

cases.title: BR-17 alert is informational only: attendance records unchanged after threshold breach notification
preconditions: Student unexcused absence rate exceeds absenceThresholdPercent 20.
Student has mix of Absent, Present, Late, and Excused records across closed sessions.
BR-17 alert event has been generated for the student-section pair.
steps: Capture beforeState: all AttendanceRecord statuses, checkInAt, and checkInMethod values for the student in the section.
Trigger BR-17 alert emission (via session close or scheduled job).
Capture afterState for all AttendanceRecord rows.
Compare beforeState and afterState field-by-field for every session in the section.
cases.expected: BR-17 exception holds: alert event and notifications are emitted but no AttendanceRecord status, checkInAt, or checkInMethod is auto-modified; informational alert does not substitute for BR-14/BR-16 manual correction paths.

cases.title: Student attendance history page shows warning when unexcused absence rate exceeds BR-17 threshold
preconditions: Student logged in on mobile-web Chromium at personal attendance history route.
Student enrolled in section where unexcused absence rate exceeds absenceThresholdPercent 20.
BR-17 absence-threshold alert has been generated for the student-section pair.
steps: Navigate to student attendance history for the at-risk section.
Verify section summary card displays unexcused absence rate percentage and configured threshold.
Observe warning alert component with localized copy explaining threshold breach per BR-17 outcome.
Confirm alert is informational only: no attendance status rows are editable by the student.
Verify other students' data is not visible on the page.
cases.expected: Student UI journey surfaces BR-17 threshold breach as a warning alert on personal attendance history; rate and threshold values match backend calculation; alert is informational and does not expose other students' records.

cases.title: Lecturer section dashboard highlights at-risk students with BR-17 absence-threshold badge
preconditions: Lecturer logged in on desktop Chromium at assigned section dashboard.
At least one enrolled student exceeds absenceThresholdPercent 20; others remain below threshold.
Section has upcoming and past sessions listed per FR-10.
steps: Navigate to class section detail or session list for the assigned section.
Locate student roster or section summary showing per-student absence rate.
Verify at-risk student row displays threshold-exceeded badge with rate and threshold tooltip.
Verify below-threshold students show no threshold badge.
Click at-risk student row and confirm detail panel shows alert timestamp and informational message without edit controls.
cases.expected: Lecturer browser journey satisfies BR-17 visibility: at-risk students are visually distinguished on section dashboard; badge reflects threshold breach; non-at-risk students remain unbadged; data scoped to lecturer-assigned section only.
